topic.what: Hybrid masking framework combining Inner Product Masking and Boolean Masking with provably secure conversion gadgets
topic.how: Proposes BM-to-IPM and IPM-to-BM conversion gadgets satisfying bit t-MIMO-SNI, optimizes IPM multiplication via Row Packing and Reduction in Rows, and demonstrates hybrid IPM-BM AES implementation
topic.why_matters: Enables practical cryptographic implementations that preserve IPM's security order amplification while achieving efficiency comparable to Boolean masking for linear operations

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fresh random bits required for IPM multiplication preprocessing
characteristics.evidence: Section 4: Row Packing and Reduction in Rows reduce randomness from quadratic to linear in n
characteristics.context: vs GD25a IPM multiplication; W is row weight parameter

characteristics.what_it_measures: XOR gate count for Boolean linear operations in AES S-box
characteristics.evidence: Table 4: x2=80 vs 402, N*x2=86 vs 536, x4=86 vs 268, Isomorphism=190 vs 1340, InvIso+Affine=235 vs 2077
characteristics.context: vs pure CM approach for 2nd-order bit-PINI masked AES S-box operations

characteristics.what_it_measures: Random bits for Boolean linear operations
characteristics.evidence: Table 4: x2=27 vs 288, N*x2=27 vs 384, x4=27 vs 192, Isomorphism=54 vs 960, InvIso+Affine=54 vs 1488
characteristics.context: vs pure CM for same security order

characteristics.evidence: Table 1: n=2,k=4 gives tb=2 vs tw=1; IPM achieves same order with fewer shares
characteristics.context: Security order amplification: 2-share IPM matches 3-share BM security

characteristics.what_it_measures: Maximum TVLA t-statistic with RNG enabled
characteristics.evidence: Figure 3c and Figure 4b: 1M traces on ARM Cortex-M4 show no leakage
characteristics.context: Non-specific fixed-vs-random TVLA; both orders pass with 1M traces

characteristics.evidence: Table 5: 2.58x overhead vs BM baseline on ARM Cortex-M4
characteristics.context: Second-order hybrid IPM-BM AES SubBytes on 46MHz Cortex-M4

characteristics.vs_baseline: Prior IPM gadgets only bit t-SNI for multiplication
characteristics.evidence: Section 3: Theorems 1-2 prove t-MIMO-SNI; Section 5: Proposition 3 and 1 prove t-PINI composition
characteristics.context: Composable security: MIMO-SNI implies PINI; PINI composites remain PINI

not_for: Low-power devices with strict randomness constraints
Scenarios without access to PRNG capable of high-dimensional uniformity
requires: Mersenne Twister or equivalent high-quality PRNG

novelty: First provably secure BM-to-IPM and IPM-to-BM conversion gadgets preserving security order amplification
First IPM multiplication optimization via Row Packing and Reduction in Rows
First end-to-end cryptographic implementation preserving IPM security order amplification in bit-probing model
First hybrid IPM-BM framework with domain separation via composable conversion
open_problems: Reducing Gadget-1 preprocessing cost (60% cycles, 80% randomness) to close gap with BM-only implementations
Extending conversion gadgets to arbitrary code-based masking beyond IPM family
Generalizing hybrid framework to hybrid CM-BM constructions
Applying hybrid IPM-BM to hardware implementations with glitch-resistant security
